input.description: List scenarios and 10-family-recipes contents

content.content:
1	# Scenario 10 — Family recipe book → meal planner: a shoebox of cards becomes a kitchen that plans the week
2	
3	> **One line.** Vasilis sends his mother's recipes — handwritten-card photos, clipped web recipes, and
4	> a voice memo — and asks THING for a recipe book by cuisine that **plans the week's meals and writes
5	> one merged shopping list** on its own every Sunday. This scenario exercises the full evolving-
6	> lifecycle template end to end and is backed by an executable live-prod runner
7	> (`10-family-recipes/run.mjs`).
8	
9	**Persona.** Vasilis, cooks for a family of four, mixes Greek and English freely. His mother's and
10	grandmother's recipes live on handwritten cards and in voice memos and are slowly being lost. He has a
11	markdown dump of recipes, a photo of a handwritten recipe card, and a voice memo from his mother. He
12	wants a real book — organized by cuisine — and the weekly mental load of "what do we eat, what do I
13	buy" taken off his plate. He is not technical.
14	
15	**Why this scenario exists.** The PROMISE under test is a **cron-driven agent synthesis that writes
16	derived rows**: every Sunday an agent reads the week's planned meals, computes a **de-duplicated
17	shopping list** (two recipes need peas → one line, 400g), and writes those rows — then pings the
18	family channel. No human asked for it that minute. Around that it wraps the full lifecycle with two
19	beats no other scenario leads with: **audio transcription → rows** and **handwritten (Greek) vision →
20	rows**. It also forces deep research into a cuisine's knowledge, an agent-processed form, a db-emitter
21	loop, mid-life self-evolution (a dietary restriction changes the plan; a dinner party scales it), and
22	an inbound channel. It closes/exposes the **`ctx.spawn`-from-app-API gap** and the **mid-life
23	table+page addition** gap.
24	
25	---
26	
27	## 1. The user flow (what the user actually does)
28	
29	| # | Step (in the UI) | What the user does |
30	|---|---|---|
31	| 1 | **Create a project** | He clicks "New project" and names it **`family-recipes`**. |
32	| 2 | **Attach the dump** | He attaches `recipes.md` (the transcribed recipes), a **photo of a handwritten recipe card** (`recipe-card.jpg`), a **printable recipe PDF** clipped from the web (`recipe.pdf`), and — if he has one — a **voice memo** from his mother. |
33	| 3 | **Ask, once** | sends the compound message below (Greek, messy). |
34	
35	> *"Σου στέλνω τις συνταγές της μάνας μου — φωτογραφίες χειρόγραφων, συνταγές από το ίντερνετ, και ένα
36	> ηχητικό. Φτιάξε μου βιβλίο ανά κουζίνα, και κάθε Κυριακή φτιάξε τα φαγητά της βδομάδας με μία ενιαία
37	> λίστα αγορών (χωρίς διπλότυπα)."*
38	
39	| 4 | **Watch it build** | THING reads the file/photo/memo, creates per-cuisine spaces, and builds the recipe app. |
40	| 5 | **See it** | He opens **`/app/family-recipes/`**: a recipe book, a meal-plan, a shopping list — real data. |
41	| 6 | **Add a recipe** | From the app he submits a new recipe via a form; an agent normalizes it into a structured row. |
42	| 7 | **Let it plan** | A Sunday cron plans the week's meals and writes a merged shopping list, then pings the family channel. |
43	| 8 | **Life changes** | Weeks later: *"ο Νίκος είναι πλέον gluten-free"* → a dietary-needs section appears and the plan adapts. Then *"hosting a dinner for 8"* → an events section that scales recipes. |
44	| 9 | **Ping from the store** | He messages *"we're out of olive oil"* → it lands on the shopping list. |
45	| 10 | **Keep updating** | *"η μουσακάς θέλει 40 λεπτά ψήσιμο, όχι 45 (ref TIME-MOUS-40)"* → the row changes. And he tests a boundary: *"order the groceries from the supermarket"* → THING refuses and hands him the scaled list instead. |
46	
47	---
48	
49	## 2. What the user expects (the contract)
50	
51	In the user's terms — success is:
52	
53	1. **"It read my cards/memo."** THING cites *his* specifics (`Μουσακάς`, `μπεσαμέλ`, `gemista`,
54	   `αρακάς`, `κεφτέδες`, `γιαγιά Αθανάσια`, `crossini`), proving it read the file *and* the card/memo.
55	2. **"I can see the book."** `/app/family-recipes/` opens and shows recipes by cuisine, a meal plan,
56	   and a shopping list — a real dashboard.
57	3. **" It learned the cuisine."** Researching a technique/substitution produced a real finding NOT in
58	   his file — it landed in a cuisine space's knowledge *and* as a row.
59	4. **"The form worked."** He added a recipe through the app; an agent normalized it into a structured
60	   row, without him chatting.
61	5. **"It plans the week for me."** The Sunday cron planned meals **and** wrote a **de-duplicated
62	   shopping list** (two recipes sharing an ingredient → one merged line), then pinged the channel.
63	6. **"It heard me at the store."** The "we're out of olive oil" message landed on the shopping list.
64	7. **"It grew with our diet."** "Gluten-free" and "dinner for 8" each produced a **new section** — a
65	   new space *and* a new table *and* a new page on the already-running app.
66	8. **"I can keep updating it."** A later message changes a real row (bake time, before→after).
67	9. **"It knows what it can't do."** "Order the groceries" → it does **not** order; it narrows to the
68	   scaled list.
69	10. **"It understood me."** It works in Greek and English; the compound Greek opener produced all the
70	    halves.
71	
72	**Anti-expectations (a failure even if the chat looks fine):**
73	- A nice summary but **no** spaces and **no** app → "it just answered me."
74	- An app that opens but is **empty** → "where are my recipes?"
75	- "Planned!" but **no** shopping-list rows → the synthesis didn't run.
76	- A shopping list with **duplicate** ingredient lines → it didn't merge.
77	- "Researched!" but **no** new row and **no** space knowledge → it didn't really research.
78	- "Noted!" on a follow-up with **no** DB change → "it didn't save it."
79	- "Ordered the groceries!" → overstep; it must NOT order.
80	
81	---
82	
83	## 3. What happens in the background (the choreography)
84	
85	Hop by hop, for maintainers:
86	
87	1. **Project creation (UI/API).** `POST /api/projects {name:"family-recipes"}`. THING runs inside it.
88	2. **Multi-modal upload.** `recipes.md` → `kind:'file'`; `recipe-card.jpg` → `kind:'image'` (handwritten
89	   card → `system-vision`, Greek OCR); `recipe.pdf` → `kind:'file'` (printable recipe, read via
90	   `readDocument`); a voice memo → `kind:'audio'` (transcription). Base64 `POST /api/uploads`.
91	3. **The message carries all attachments over the WS path**; the HTTP `/message` route drops them.
92	4. **THING delegates the read.** File ids → **`system-files/dispatch`** (md → reader; handwritten image
93	   → `system-vision`; audio → transcription). Extracted facts (incl. from the card and memo) return to
94	   THING.
95	5. **THING plans and delegates the build.** (a) Per-cuisine **spaces** (`ελληνική`, `ιταλική`,
96	   `household-preferences`) via `build_specialist`, **live-registered**. (b)
97	   **`system-appbuilder/automator`** authors the live recipe app.
98	6. **The automator authors INTO the live project:** `writeProjectTable(name, schema, rows)` (seeds the
99	   recipes), `writeProjectApi`, `writeProjectPage` (a **recipe-book** + **meal-plan** +
100	   **shopping-list** page). `POST /app/family-recipes/build` compiles; `GET /app/family-recipes/`
101	   serves real HTML.
102	7. **Deep research (Act II).** "What's an authentic substitution / technique for X" routes to
103	   **`system-research/researcher`** (`webSearch`/`webFetch`). Findings land in the cuisine space's
104	   **knowledge** *and* as a `substitutions` row via `db.insert`, absent from the seed.
105	8. **Agent-processed form (Act III).** An "add recipe" **page form** → `POST
106	   /app/family-recipes/api/recipe-create` → `ctx.db.insert('recipes', …)`. That insert fires
107	   `project/db.recipes.insert` → an **event hook** with `trigger: '<space>/agent#normalize'` → an
108	   **agent turn** that normalizes the raw text into a structured row. **`ctx.spawn` from an app API is
109	   a known no-op**; the db-insert→hook path is the working one and what this asserts.
110	9. **Cron-driven synthesis → derived rows (Act IV).** A `cron` hook (`every:'7d'`, `trigger:
111	   '<space>/agent#weekly_plan'`) reads the recipe book, **plans the week**, computes a
112	   **de-duplicated shopping list** (shared ingredients merged with summed quantities), writes
113	   `shopping_list` rows, and pings the family channel; the runner triggers it via
114	   `pod.runEmitter`/`runHook`. This is the headline — an agent authoring **derived** rows on a schedule.
115	10. **Self-evolution (Act V).** "Ο Νίκος είναι gluten-free" adds a NEW `dietary-needs` space (GF
116	    knowledge) → tags/flags recipes and the meal-plan adapts. "Hosting a dinner for 8" adds a NEW
117	    `events` space + `event_menu` table + a **scaling page** — all on the **already-built** app; the
118	    manifest **grows** post-build.
119	11. **Inbound + outbound (Act VI).** `installSpace('integration-demo')` (keyless test source; a real
120	    Telegram/WhatsApp space in production) raises a **consent card** the user approves. A signed
121	    `POST /api/inbound/<path>` ("we're out of olive oil") → verify→emit → event hook → agent → a
122	    `shopping_list` row. The agent also posts the weekly plan to the family channel via
123	    **`callConnection`** (gated `connections:use`).
124	12. **Later updates + restraint (Act VII).** A follow-up uses `db.update` to fix the moussaka bake time
125	    (NEW token `TIME-MOUS-40`, before/after). "Order the groceries from the supermarket" → THING
126	    **refuses/narrows**: no ordering; it offers the scaled list.
127	
128	Everything above is authored by the model into the user's own project — no engineer touches a file.
129	
130	---
131	
132	## 4. User stories
133	
134	- **US-1 — Ingest multi-modal (incl. Greek card + voice).** *As a home cook, I want to send cards,
135	  files, and a voice memo.* **Accept:** `system-files`/`system-vision` delegated; ≥3 recipe-specific
136	  facts cited (incl. at least one only the card/memo carries).
137	- **US-2 — See the book.** *As a home cook, I want a real app.* **Accept:** app `built:true` with
138	  tables + ≥1 page; `/app/family-recipes/` → 200 HTML.
139	- **US-3 — My recipes are in it.** *As a home cook, I want my recipes stored by cuisine.* **Accept:**
140	  a recipes table holds the file's recipes, contents matching the file.
141	- **US-4 — It learns the cuisine.** *As a home cook, I want substitutions/techniques researched.*
142	  **Accept:** `system-research` delegated, `webSearch`/`webFetch` observed; a researched row absent
143	  from the seed lands in `substitutions` + a cuisine space's knowledge.
144	- **US-5 — The form is alive.** *As a home cook, I want to add a recipe through the app.* **Accept:**
145	  a `POST` to the form API fires an agent turn and a normalized recipe row lands (before/after with a
146	  NEW token).
147	- **US-6 — It plans the week.** *As a home cook, I want Sunday's plan + one merged shopping list.*
148	  **Accept:** triggering the cron emitter writes `meal_plan` rows **and** a **de-duplicated**
149	  `shopping_list` (no duplicate ingredient lines; shared ingredients merged), then a channel ping.
150	- **US-7 — It hears me at the store.** *As a home cook, I want to ping the list from a channel.*
151	  **Accept:** install consent approved; a signed inbound webhook → agent → a `shopping_list` row.
152	- **US-8 — It grows with our diet.** *As a home cook, I want new needs to add sections.* **Accept:**
153	  "gluten-free" and "dinner for 8" each add a NEW space + NEW table + NEW page to the running app
154	  (manifest grows after the initial build).
155	- **US-9 — Keep it current.** *As a home cook, I want to update it by just telling it.* **Accept:** a
156	  follow-up changes a real row (bake time, before/after).
157	- **US-10 — It knows its limits.** *As a home cook, I want it to not order for me.* **Accept:** "order
158	  the groceries" → no ordering (trace clean); the scaled list offered.
159	- **US-11 — Understand me.** *As a cook who mixes Greek/English, I want it to work in either.*
160	  **Accept:** a Greek follow-up updates a row; the compound Greek opener produced all halves.
161	
162	---
163	
164	## 5. Feature coverage (tick what this scenario exercises)
165	
166	- THING routing: [x] answer [x] research [x] build space [x] app-4a (automator) [ ] app-4b (build_app)
167	  [ ] code (engineer) [ ] memory [x] install+automate [x] compound request [x] provided-info shortcut
168	  [x] restraint/refusal [x] multilingual
169	- Spaces: [x] create per-part [x] live-registered/delegatable [x] no-clobber re-add
170	- Event pipeline: [x] webhook (inbound) [x] cron [x] db (recipes.insert) [ ] internal ·
171	  [x] code-handler hook [x] agent-trigger hook · [ ] code nodes [ ] forEach · [x] project functions ·
172	  [x] loop guard [x] payload validation [x] emitEvent
173	- Consent/caps: [x] @consent [x] installSpace approve [x] fail-closed headless
174	  [x] capability gating (`db:write`, `events:emit`, `connections:use`, `store:install`)
175	- Store/integrations: [x] discovery [x] install a space [x] callConnection [x] inbound webhook
176	  [x] integration-demo source (keyless; telegram is the prod target)
177	- Project-app: [x] writeProjectTable(+rows seed) [x] writeProjectPage/Api [x] db:write later-update
178	  [x] app build [x] /app/<id>/ serving [x] app data API [x] **mid-life table+page addition**
179	- Attachments: [x] upload [x] readDocument [x] attachmentIds to a specialist [x] **vision (handwritten Greek)** / **audio**
180	- Pod lifecycle: [ ] restart→auto-resume (covered by 05) [x] cold-wake [ ] event storm [x] worker containment (api handler)
181	- Cross-cutting: [x] edge cases/errors [x] performance [x] budget (direct Azure keys)
182	
183	---
184	
185	## 6. Acceptance criteria (the Acts)
186	
187	The runner (`10-family-recipes/run.mjs`) drives these and asserts on the **trace + real pod state**.
188	Acts here match the runner 1:1.
189	
190	| Act | Asserts (trace + real state) | Stories |
191	|---|---|---|
192	| **I — Ingest & build** | `system-files`/`system-vision` delegated; ≥3 recipe facts cited; ≥2 per-cuisine spaces; app `built:true` with tables + ≥1 page; `/app/family-recipes/` → 200 HTML; a recipes table seeded with file rows (content tokens match) | US-1,2,3,11 |
193	| **II — Deep research → knowledge + DB** | `system-research` delegated + `webSearch`/`webFetch` observed; a researched substitution **absent from the seed** lands as a row in `substitutions`; a cuisine space answers a follow-up from researched knowledge | US-4 |
194	| **III — Agent-processed form** | a `POST` to `/app/family-recipes/api/<form>` returns ≥202; an **agent turn fires** (via `db.insert`→emitter→hook, not `ctx.spawn`); a normalized recipe row with a NEW token lands (before/after) | US-5 |
195	| **IV — Cron synthesis → derived rows** | a `cron` hook exists; `runEmitter`/`runHook` produces an agent turn that writes `meal_plan` rows **and** a **de-duplicated** `shopping_list` (shared ingredients merged — no duplicate ingredient lines); a channel ping (callConnection yield) observed | US-6 |
196	| **V — Self-evolution** | "gluten-free" + "dinner for 8" each add a NEW space (live-registered) **and** the app manifest gains ≥1 NEW table and ≥1 NEW page beyond Act I's manifest (mid-life growth) | US-8 |
197	| **VI — Inbound + outbound** | `installSpace` consent approved; a signed inbound webhook → `{events ≥1}` (bad signature → 401/0 events); an agent/hook writes a `shopping_list` row (before/after); a `callConnection` yield observed | US-7 |
198	| **VII — Update + restraint + multilingual** | a Greek follow-up changes a real row (bake time `TIME-MOUS-40`, before/after); "order the groceries" → no ordering (trace clean) + the scaled list offered | US-9,10,11 |
199	| **Edges** | idempotent re-ask doesn't clobber spaces; malformed inbound → 0 events; a failing automation surfaces its error; zero unrecovered eval/typecheck errors on THING's own turns | — |
200	
201	### Performance targets
202	| Metric | Target |
203	|---|---|
204	| Ingest → THING plan | < 90 s |
205	| Whole build (spaces + app + seeded data) | < 15 min |
206	| `/app/family-recipes/` first byte | < 3 s |
207	| Research turn → substitution row | < 3 min |
208	| Form POST → recipe row | < 90 s |
209	| Cron trigger → meal_plan + shopping_list rows | < 3 min |
210	| Later-update message → row changed | < 90 s |
211	| Eval/typecheck errors (unrecovered, on THING's own turns) | 0 |
212	
213	---
214	
215	## 7. What this scenario is really testing (and the gaps it closes/exposes)
216	
217	This is the scenario that forces a **cron-driven agent synthesis writing derived rows** — the clearest
218	"it does the weekly thinking for me" claim — and the only one that leads with **audio → rows** and
219	**handwritten (Greek) vision → rows**. Three gaps are in play:
220	
221	1. **Cron → agent synthesis → derived rows.** A scheduled emitter must wake an agent that **reads the
222	   book, plans, and authors new rows** (a de-duplicated shopping list), not merely ping. US-6 is the
223	   headline; the de-duplication makes it a real synthesis, not a copy.
224	2. **Agent-processed form (the `ctx.spawn` gap).** `ctx.spawn` from an app API is a **known no-op**;
225	   the working path is a `db:insert` emitter → event hook with a `trigger`. US-5 asserts the working
226	   path and documents the gap.
227	3. **Mid-life self-evolution.** No prior scenario adds a **new table + page** to an **already-built**
228	   app from a later turn. US-8 asserts the manifest grows after Act I — here driven by a dietary
229	   change and an event, the natural shape of a living kitchen.
230	
231	Also: **audio transcription** and **handwritten Greek OCR** are exercised for the first time — the
232	runner proves the path always, and the content assertion when a real voice memo / real card photo is
233	present.
234	
235	A recovered `typecheck_error`/`eval_error` inside a delegated specialist is the retry surface, not a
236	failure: hard-assert the **deliverable**, record recovered errors as a metric + note.
237	
238	---
239	
240	## 8. Running it
241	
242	```bash
243	cd sdk/org/scenarios/harness
244	node smoke.mjs                         # prove harness + prod healthy first
245	node ../10-family-recipes/run.mjs      # fresh; writes 10-family-recipes/results/report.md
246	node ../10-family-recipes/run.mjs --reuse # reuse the cached user + project
247	```
248	
249	The runner provisions a disposable prod user, creates `family-recipes`, uploads `fixtures/recipes.md`
250	+ `fixtures/recipe-card.jpg` + `fixtures/recipe.pdf` (+ a voice memo if `fixtures/voice-memo.m4a` is
251	present — audio is otherwise skipped with a note), sends the compound Greek message over the WS path,
252	drives the research / form / cron-plan / evolution / inbound / follow-up beats, and checkpoints per Act
253	to `results/checkpoint.json`.
254	
255	> **Vision/audio honesty:** `fixtures/recipe-card.jpg` is a real photo of a handwritten recipe card
256	> (it supersedes the tiny `recipe-card.png` placeholder) and `fixtures/recipe.pdf` is a real printable
257	> recipe with selectable text — together they exercise **handwritten Greek OCR → rows** (via
258	> `system-vision`) and the **`readDocument` path**. To also assert **audio transcription → rows**, drop
259	> a real `voice-memo.m4a` at `fixtures/` before running. The runner asserts the delegate path always,
260	> and the content assertion when a real artifact is present.
261	
262	## Actual results
263	
264	_Filled in by the runner — paste from `results/report.md` after a run._
265

# Scenario 10 — Family recipe book → meal planner: a shoebox of cards becomes a kitchen that plans the week

> **One line.** Vasilis sends his mother's recipes — handwritten-card photos, clipped web recipes, and
> a voice memo — and asks THING for a recipe book by cuisine that **plans the week's meals and writes
> one merged shopping list** on its own every Sunday. This scenario exercises the full evolving-
> lifecycle template end to end and is backed by an executable live-prod runner
> (`10-family-recipes/run.mjs`).

**Persona.** Vasilis, cooks for a family of four, mixes Greek and English freely. His mother's and
grandmother's recipes live on handwritten cards and in voice memos and are slowly being lost. He has a
markdown dump of recipes, a photo of a handwritten recipe card, and a voice memo from his mother. He
wants a real book — organized by cuisine — and the weekly mental load of "what do we eat, what do I
buy" taken off his plate. He is not technical.

**Why this scenario exists.** The PROMISE under test is a **cron-driven agent synthesis that writes
derived rows**: every Sunday an agent reads the week's planned meals, computes a **de-duplicated
shopping list** (two recipes need peas → one line, 400g), and writes those rows — then pings the
family channel. No human asked for it that minute. Around that it wraps the full lifecycle with two
beats no other scenario leads with: **audio transcription → rows** and **handwritten (Greek) vision →
rows**. It also forces deep research into a cuisine's knowledge, an agent-processed form, a db-emitter
loop, mid-life self-evolution (a dietary restriction changes the plan; a dinner party scales it), and
an inbound channel. It closes/exposes the **`ctx.spawn`-from-app-API gap** and the **mid-life
table+page addition** gap.

---

## 1. The user flow (what the user actually does)

| # | Step (in the UI) | What the user does |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Create a project** | He clicks "New project" and names it **`family-recipes`**. |
| 2 | **Attach the dump** | He attaches `recipes.md` (the transcribed recipes), a **photo of a handwritten recipe card** (`recipe-card.jpg`), a **printable recipe PDF** clipped from the web (`recipe.pdf`), and — if he has one — a **voice memo** from his mother. |
| 3 | **Ask, once** | sends the compound message below (Greek, messy). |

> *"Σου στέλνω τις συνταγές της μάνας μου — φωτογραφίες χειρόγραφων, συνταγές από το ίντερνετ, και ένα
> ηχητικό. Φτιάξε μου βιβλίο ανά κουζίνα, και κάθε Κυριακή φτιάξε τα φαγητά της βδομάδας με μία ενιαία
> λίστα αγορών (χωρίς διπλότυπα)."*

| 4 | **Watch it build** | THING reads the file/photo/memo, creates per-cuisine spaces, and builds the recipe app. |
| 5 | **See it** | He opens **`/app/family-recipes/`**: a recipe book, a meal-plan, a shopping list — real data. |
| 6 | **Add a recipe** | From the app he submits a new recipe via a form; an agent normalizes it into a structured row. |
| 7 | **Let it plan** | A Sunday cron plans the week's meals and writes a merged shopping list, then pings the family channel. |
| 8 | **Life changes** | Weeks later: *"ο Νίκος είναι πλέον gluten-free"* → a dietary-needs section appears and the plan adapts. Then *"hosting a dinner for 8"* → an events section that scales recipes. |
| 9 | **Ping from the store** | He messages *"we're out of olive oil"* → it lands on the shopping list. |
| 10 | **Keep updating** | *"η μουσακάς θέλει 40 λεπτά ψήσιμο, όχι 45 (ref TIME-MOUS-40)"* → the row changes. And he tests a boundary: *"order the groceries from the supermarket"* → THING refuses and hands him the scaled list instead. |

---

## 2. What the user expects (the contract)

In the user's terms — success is:

1. **"It read my cards/memo."** THING cites *his* specifics (`Μουσακάς`, `μπεσαμέλ`, `gemista`,
   `αρακάς`, `κεφτέδες`, `γιαγιά Αθανάσια`, `crossini`), proving it read the file *and* the card/memo.
2. **"I can see the book."** `/app/family-recipes/` opens and shows recipes by cuisine, a meal plan,
   and a shopping list — a real dashboard.
3. **" It learned the cuisine."** Researching a technique/substitution produced a real finding NOT in
   his file — it landed in a cuisine space's knowledge *and* as a row.
4. **"The form worked."** He added a recipe through the app; an agent normalized it into a structured
   row, without him chatting.
5. **"It plans the week for me."** The Sunday cron planned meals **and** wrote a **de-duplicated
   shopping list** (two recipes sharing an ingredient → one merged line), then pinged the channel.
6. **"It heard me at the store."** The "we're out of olive oil" message landed on the shopping list.
7. **"It grew with our diet."** "Gluten-free" and "dinner for 8" each produced a **new section** — a
   new space *and* a new table *and* a new page on the already-running app.
8. **"I can keep updating it."** A later message changes a real row (bake time, before→after).
9. **"It knows what it can't do."** "Order the groceries" → it does **not** order; it narrows to the
   scaled list.
10. **"It understood me."** It works in Greek and English; the compound Greek opener produced all the
    halves.

**Anti-expectations (a failure even if the chat looks fine):**
- A nice summary but **no** spaces and **no** app → "it just answered me."
- An app that opens but is **empty** → "where are my recipes?"
- "Planned!" but **no** shopping-list rows → the synthesis didn't run.
- A shopping list with **duplicate** ingredient lines → it didn't merge.
- "Researched!" but **no** new row and **no** space knowledge → it didn't really research.
- "Noted!" on a follow-up with **no** DB change → "it didn't save it."
- "Ordered the groceries!" → overstep; it must NOT order.

---

## 3. What happens in the background (the choreography)

Hop by hop, for maintainers:

1. **Project creation (UI/API).** `POST /api/projects {name:"family-recipes"}`. THING runs inside it.
2. **Multi-modal upload.** `recipes.md` → `kind:'file'`; `recipe-card.jpg` → `kind:'image'` (handwritten
   card → `system-vision`, Greek OCR); `recipe.pdf` → `kind:'file'` (printable recipe, read via
   `readDocument`); a voice memo → `kind:'audio'` (transcription). Base64 `POST /api/uploads`.
3. **The message carries all attachments over the WS path**; the HTTP `/message` route drops them.
4. **THING delegates the read.** File ids → **`system-files/dispatch`** (md → reader; handwritten image
   → `system-vision`; audio → transcription). Extracted facts (incl. from the card and memo) return to
   THING.
5. **THING plans and delegates the build.** (a) Per-cuisine **spaces** (`ελληνική`, `ιταλική`,
   `household-preferences`) via `build_specialist`, **live-registered**. (b)
   **`system-appbuilder/automator`** authors the live recipe app.
6. **The automator authors INTO the live project:** `writeProjectTable(name, schema, rows)` (seeds the
   recipes), `writeProjectApi`, `writeProjectPage` (a **recipe-book** + **meal-plan** +
   **shopping-list** page). `POST /app/family-recipes/build` compiles; `GET /app/family-recipes/`
   serves real HTML.
7. **Deep research (Act II).** "What's an authentic substitution / technique for X" routes to
   **`system-research/researcher`** (`webSearch`/`webFetch`). Findings land in the cuisine space's
   **knowledge** *and* as a `substitutions` row via `db.insert`, absent from the seed.
8. **Agent-processed form (Act III).** An "add recipe" **page form** → `POST
   /app/family-recipes/api/recipe-create` → `ctx.db.insert('recipes', …)`. That insert fires
   `project/db.recipes.insert` → an **event hook** with `trigger: '<space>/agent#normalize'` → an
   **agent turn** that normalizes the raw text into a structured row. **`ctx.spawn` from an app API is
   a known no-op**; the db-insert→hook path is the working one and what this asserts.
9. **Cron-driven synthesis → derived rows (Act IV).** A `cron` hook (`every:'7d'`, `trigger:
   '<space>/agent#weekly_plan'`) reads the recipe book, **plans the week**, computes a
   **de-duplicated shopping list** (shared ingredients merged with summed quantities), writes
   `shopping_list` rows, and pings the family channel; the runner triggers it via
   `pod.runEmitter`/`runHook`. This is the headline — an agent authoring **derived** rows on a schedule.
10. **Self-evolution (Act V).** "Ο Νίκος είναι gluten-free" adds a NEW `dietary-needs` space (GF
    knowledge) → tags/flags recipes and the meal-plan adapts. "Hosting a dinner for 8" adds a NEW
    `events` space + `event_menu` table + a **scaling page** — all on the **already-built** app; the
    manifest **grows** post-build.
11. **Inbound + outbound (Act VI).** `installSpace('integration-demo')` (keyless test source; a real
    Telegram/WhatsApp space in production) raises a **consent card** the user approves. A signed
    `POST /api/inbound/<path>` ("we're out of olive oil") → verify→emit → event hook → agent → a
    `shopping_list` row. The agent also posts the weekly plan to the family channel via
    **`callConnection`** (gated `connections:use`).
12. **Later updates + restraint (Act VII).** A follow-up uses `db.update` to fix the moussaka bake time
    (NEW token `TIME-MOUS-40`, before/after). "Order the groceries from the supermarket" → THING
    **refuses/narrows**: no ordering; it offers the scaled list.

Everything above is authored by the model into the user's own project — no engineer touches a file.

---

## 4. User stories

- **US-1 — Ingest multi-modal (incl. Greek card + voice).** *As a home cook, I want to send cards,
  files, and a voice memo.* **Accept:** `system-files`/`system-vision` delegated; ≥3 recipe-specific
  facts cited (incl. at least one only the card/memo carries).
- **US-2 — See the book.** *As a home cook, I want a real app.* **Accept:** app `built:true` with
  tables + ≥1 page; `/app/family-recipes/` → 200 HTML.
- **US-3 — My recipes are in it.** *As a home cook, I want my recipes stored by cuisine.* **Accept:**
  a recipes table holds the file's recipes, contents matching the file.
- **US-4 — It learns the cuisine.** *As a home cook, I want substitutions/techniques researched.*
  **Accept:** `system-research` delegated, `webSearch`/`webFetch` observed; a researched row absent
  from the seed lands in `substitutions` + a cuisine space's knowledge.
- **US-5 — The form is alive.** *As a home cook, I want to add a recipe through the app.* **Accept:**
  a `POST` to the form API fires an agent turn and a normalized recipe row lands (before/after with a
  NEW token).
- **US-6 — It plans the week.** *As a home cook, I want Sunday's plan + one merged shopping list.*
  **Accept:** triggering the cron emitter writes `meal_plan` rows **and** a **de-duplicated**
  `shopping_list` (no duplicate ingredient lines; shared ingredients merged), then a channel ping.
- **US-7 — It hears me at the store.** *As a home cook, I want to ping the list from a channel.*
  **Accept:** install consent approved; a signed inbound webhook → agent → a `shopping_list` row.
- **US-8 — It grows with our diet.** *As a home cook, I want new needs to add sections.* **Accept:**
  "gluten-free" and "dinner for 8" each add a NEW space + NEW table + NEW page to the running app
  (manifest grows after the initial build).
- **US-9 — Keep it current.** *As a home cook, I want to update it by just telling it.* **Accept:** a
  follow-up changes a real row (bake time, before/after).
- **US-10 — It knows its limits.** *As a home cook, I want it to not order for me.* **Accept:** "order
  the groceries" → no ordering (trace clean); the scaled list offered.
- **US-11 — Understand me.** *As a cook who mixes Greek/English, I want it to work in either.*
  **Accept:** a Greek follow-up updates a row; the compound Greek opener produced all halves.

---

## 5. Feature coverage (tick what this scenario exercises)

- THING routing: [x] answer [x] research [x] build space [x] app-4a (automator) [ ] app-4b (build_app)
  [ ] code (engineer) [ ] memory [x] install+automate [x] compound request [x] provided-info shortcut
  [x] restraint/refusal [x] multilingual
- Spaces: [x] create per-part [x] live-registered/delegatable [x] no-clobber re-add
- Event pipeline: [x] webhook (inbound) [x] cron [x] db (recipes.insert) [ ] internal ·
  [x] code-handler hook [x] agent-trigger hook · [ ] code nodes [ ] forEach · [x] project functions ·
  [x] loop guard [x] payload validation [x] emitEvent
- Consent/caps: [x] @consent [x] installSpace approve [x] fail-closed headless
  [x] capability gating (`db:write`, `events:emit`, `connections:use`, `store:install`)
- Store/integrations: [x] discovery [x] install a space [x] callConnection [x] inbound webhook
  [x] integration-demo source (keyless; telegram is the prod target)
- Project-app: [x] writeProjectTable(+rows seed) [x] writeProjectPage/Api [x] db:write later-update
  [x] app build [x] /app/<id>/ serving [x] app data API [x] **mid-life table+page addition**
- Attachments: [x] upload [x] readDocument [x] attachmentIds to a specialist [x] **vision (handwritten Greek)** / **audio**
- Pod lifecycle: [ ] restart→auto-resume (covered by 05) [x] cold-wake [ ] event storm [x] worker containment (api handler)
- Cross-cutting: [x] edge cases/errors [x] performance [x] budget (direct Azure keys)

---

## 6. Acceptance criteria (the Acts)

The runner (`10-family-recipes/run.mjs`) drives these and asserts on the **trace + real pod state**.
Acts here match the runner 1:1.

| Act | Asserts (trace + real state) | Stories |
|---|---|---|
| **I — Ingest & build** | `system-files`/`system-vision` delegated; ≥3 recipe facts cited; ≥2 per-cuisine spaces; app `built:true` with tables + ≥1 page; `/app/family-recipes/` → 200 HTML; a recipes table seeded with file rows (content tokens match) | US-1,2,3,11 |
| **II — Deep research → knowledge + DB** | `system-research` delegated + `webSearch`/`webFetch` observed; a researched substitution **absent from the seed** lands as a row in `substitutions`; a cuisine space answers a follow-up from researched knowledge | US-4 |
| **III — Agent-processed form** | a `POST` to `/app/family-recipes/api/<form>` returns ≥202; an **agent turn fires** (via `db.insert`→emitter→hook, not `ctx.spawn`); a normalized recipe row with a NEW token lands (before/after) | US-5 |
| **IV — Cron synthesis → derived rows** | a `cron` hook exists; `runEmitter`/`runHook` produces an agent turn that writes `meal_plan` rows **and** a **de-duplicated** `shopping_list` (shared ingredients merged — no duplicate ingredient lines); a channel ping (callConnection yield) observed | US-6 |
| **V — Self-evolution** | "gluten-free" + "dinner for 8" each add a NEW space (live-registered) **and** the app manifest gains ≥1 NEW table and ≥1 NEW page beyond Act I's manifest (mid-life growth) | US-8 |
| **VI — Inbound + outbound** | `installSpace` consent approved; a signed inbound webhook → `{events ≥1}` (bad signature → 401/0 events); an agent/hook writes a `shopping_list` row (before/after); a `callConnection` yield observed | US-7 |
| **VII — Update + restraint + multilingual** | a Greek follow-up changes a real row (bake time `TIME-MOUS-40`, before/after); "order the groceries" → no ordering (trace clean) + the scaled list offered | US-9,10,11 |
| **Edges** | idempotent re-ask doesn't clobber spaces; malformed inbound → 0 events; a failing automation surfaces its error; zero unrecovered eval/typecheck errors on THING's own turns | — |

### Performance targets
| Metric | Target |
|---|---|
| Ingest → THING plan | < 90 s |
| Whole build (spaces + app + seeded data) | < 15 min |
| `/app/family-recipes/` first byte | < 3 s |
| Research turn → substitution row | < 3 min |
| Form POST → recipe row | < 90 s |
| Cron trigger → meal_plan + shopping_list rows | < 3 min |
| Later-update message → row changed | < 90 s |
| Eval/typecheck errors (unrecovered, on THING's own turns) | 0 |

---

## 7. What this scenario is really testing (and the gaps it closes/exposes)

This is the scenario that forces a **cron-driven agent synthesis writing derived rows** — the clearest
"it does the weekly thinking for me" claim — and the only one that leads with **audio → rows** and
**handwritten (Greek) vision → rows**. Three gaps are in play:

1. **Cron → agent synthesis → derived rows.** A scheduled emitter must wake an agent that **reads the
   book, plans, and authors new rows** (a de-duplicated shopping list), not merely ping. US-6 is the
   headline; the de-duplication makes it a real synthesis, not a copy.
2. **Agent-processed form (the `ctx.spawn` gap).** `ctx.spawn` from an app API is a **known no-op**;
   the working path is a `db:insert` emitter → event hook with a `trigger`. US-5 asserts the working
   path and documents the gap.
3. **Mid-life self-evolution.** No prior scenario adds a **new table + page** to an **already-built**
   app from a later turn. US-8 asserts the manifest grows after Act I — here driven by a dietary
   change and an event, the natural shape of a living kitchen.

Also: **audio transcription** and **handwritten Greek OCR** are exercised for the first time — the
runner proves the path always, and the content assertion when a real voice memo / real card photo is
present.

A recovered `typecheck_error`/`eval_error` inside a delegated specialist is the retry surface, not a
failure: hard-assert the **deliverable**, record recovered errors as a metric + note.

---

## 8. Running it

```bash
cd sdk/org/scenarios/harness
node smoke.mjs                         # prove harness + prod healthy first
node ../10-family-recipes/run.mjs      # fresh; writes 10-family-recipes/results/report.md
node ../10-family-recipes/run.mjs --reuse # reuse the cached user + project
```

The runner provisions a disposable prod user, creates `family-recipes`, uploads `fixtures/recipes.md`
+ `fixtures/recipe-card.jpg` + `fixtures/recipe.pdf` (+ a voice memo if `fixtures/voice-memo.m4a` is
present — audio is otherwise skipped with a note), sends the compound Greek message over the WS path,
drives the research / form / cron-plan / evolution / inbound / follow-up beats, and checkpoints per Act
to `results/checkpoint.json`.

> **Vision/audio honesty:** `fixtures/recipe-card.jpg` is a real photo of a handwritten recipe card
> (it supersedes the tiny `recipe-card.png` placeholder) and `fixtures/recipe.pdf` is a real printable
> recipe with selectable text — together they exercise **handwritten Greek OCR → rows** (via
> `system-vision`) and the **`readDocument` path**. To also assert **audio transcription → rows**, drop
> a real `voice-memo.m4a` at `fixtures/` before running. The runner asserts the delegate path always,
> and the content assertion when a real artifact is present.

## Actual results

_Filled in by the runner — paste from `results/report.md` after a run._

commands.name: claude-hud:setup
commands.description: (claude-hud) Configure claude-hud as your statusline

commands.name: claude-hud:configure
commands.description: (claude-hud) Configure HUD display options (layout, language, presets, display elements) while preserving advanced manual overrides

commands.name: howl:configure
commands.description: (howl) Configure Howl statusline display preset with interactive guidance and preview

commands.name: howl:customize
commands.description: (howl) Customize Howl statusline with fine-grained metric toggles and priority ordering

commands.name: howl:setup
commands.description: (howl) This skill should be used when the user asks to "install howl", "set up the statusline", "configure howl", "enable the HUD", or "install the statusline binary". It downloads the Howl binary and configures Claude Code to use it.

commands.name: howl:threshold
commands.description: (howl) Customize Howl color thresholds and behavior triggers interactively

commands.name: design-sync
commands.description: Push a React design system to claude.ai/design. This runs a converter that bundles the real component code (from Storybook or a bare package) and uploads it. Use when the user runs /design-sync or says "sync my design system to Claude Design".

commands.name: dataviz
commands.description: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".

commands.name: update-config
commands.description: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.

commands.name: verify
commands.description: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.

commands.name: debug
commands.description: Enable debug logging for this session and help diagnose issues

commands.name: code-review
commands.description: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.

commands.name: simplify
commands.description: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.

commands.name: batch
commands.description: Research and plan a large-scale change, then execute it in parallel across 5–30 isolated worktree agents that each open a PR.

commands.name: fewer-permission-prompts
commands.description: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.

commands.name: doctor
commands.description: Health-check the user's Claude Code setup and fix issues: diagnose installation health — what the `claude doctor` terminal diagnostics cover — from local data (duplicate or leftover installs, PATH, unparseable settings files, broken or colliding agent definitions); find unused skills, MCP servers, and plugins versus their context cost and disable dead weight; deduplicate local CLAUDE.md files against checked-in ones; trim checked-in CLAUDE.md files by cutting content a session could derive from the codebase (directory layouts, tech-stack lists, architecture overviews) while keeping gotchas, rationale, and non-standard conventions; migrate always-loaded CLAUDE.md guidance into lazy skills and nested CLAUDE.md files; flag slow hooks and context-heavy extensions; check the installed version is current; make auto mode the default permission mode; and pre-approve frequently denied read-only commands. Use when the user asks for a doctor run, checkup, audit, tune-up, or cleanup of their Claude Code setup or configuration.

commands.name: loop
commands.description: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace.

commands.name: schedule
commands.description: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule.

commands.name: claude-api
commands.description:
Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).

commands.name: run
commands.description: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

commands.name: run-skill-generator
commands.description: Author or improve the run-<unit> skill — a per-project skill that tells agents how to build, launch, and drive this project's app. Use when the user asks to set up the project, get it running, write run instructions, or verify build/run steps work from a clean environment.

commands.name: update-config
commands.description: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.

commands.name: verify
commands.description: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.

commands.name: debug
commands.description: Enable debug logging for this session and help diagnose issues

commands.name: code-review
commands.description: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.

commands.name: simplify
commands.description: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.

commands.name: batch
commands.description: Research and plan a large-scale change, then execute it in parallel across 5–30 isolated worktree agents that each open a PR.

commands.name: fewer-permission-prompts
commands.description: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.

commands.name: doctor
commands.description: Health-check the user's Claude Code setup and fix issues: diagnose installation health — what the `claude doctor` terminal diagnostics cover — from local data (duplicate or leftover installs, PATH, unparseable settings files, broken or colliding agent definitions); find unused skills, MCP servers, and plugins versus their context cost and disable dead weight; deduplicate local CLAUDE.md files against checked-in ones; trim checked-in CLAUDE.md files by cutting content a session could derive from the codebase (directory layouts, tech-stack lists, architecture overviews) while keeping gotchas, rationale, and non-standard conventions; migrate always-loaded CLAUDE.md guidance into lazy skills and nested CLAUDE.md files; flag slow hooks and context-heavy extensions; check the installed version is current; make auto mode the default permission mode; and pre-approve frequently denied read-only commands. Use when the user asks for a doctor run, checkup, audit, tune-up, or cleanup of their Claude Code setup or configuration.

commands.name: loop
commands.description: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace.

commands.name: schedule
commands.description: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule.

commands.name: claude-api
commands.description:
Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).

commands.name: run
commands.description: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

commands.name: run-skill-generator
commands.description: Author or improve the run-<unit> skill — a per-project skill that tells agents how to build, launch, and drive this project's app. Use when the user asks to set up the project, get it running, write run instructions, or verify build/run steps work from a clean environment.

commands.name: agents
commands.description: (removed) Ask Claude to create/manage subagents, or edit .claude/agents/

commands.name: clear
commands.description: Start a new session with empty context; previous session stays on disk (resumable with /resume)

commands.name: color
commands.description: Set the prompt bar color for this session

commands.name: compact
commands.description: Free up context by summarizing the conversation so far

commands.name: config
commands.description: Set a setting by key

commands.name: context
commands.description: Show current context usage

commands.name: effort
commands.description: Set effort level for model usage

commands.name: fast
commands.description: Toggle fast mode (Opus 4.8)

commands.name: heapdump
commands.description: Dump the JS heap to ~/Desktop

commands.name: init
commands.description: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation

commands.name: mcp
commands.description: Manage MCP servers

commands.name: model
commands.description: Set the AI model for Claude Code

commands.name: __remote-workflow
commands.description: Run the workflow script delivered in this session environment (server-launched sessions only)

commands.name: reload-skills
commands.description: Pick up skills added or changed on disk during this session

commands.name: rename
commands.description: Rename the current conversation

commands.name: review
commands.description: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review

commands.name: security-review
commands.description: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

commands.name: usage-credits
commands.description: Configure usage credits to keep working when you hit a limit

commands.name: extra-usage
commands.description: Renamed to /usage-credits

commands.name: usage
commands.description: Show session cost, plan usage, and what's contributing to your limits

commands.name: insights
commands.description: Generate a report analyzing your Claude Code sessions

commands.name: recap
commands.description: Generate a one-line session recap now

commands.name: goal
commands.description: Set a goal — keep working until the condition is met

commands.name: design
commands.description: Grant or revoke Claude agent access to your Design projects

commands.name: design-consent
commands.description: Grant Claude agent access to your Design projects

commands.name: design-revoke
commands.description: Revoke Claude agent access to your Design projects

commands.name: team-onboarding
commands.description: Help teammates ramp on Claude Code with a guide from your usage

commands.name: design
commands.description: Use Claude Design (claude.ai/design) from this conversation — create projects, read existing ones, and write decks/prototypes/mockups using your team's design system.

1	# Βιβλίο συνταγών της οικογένειας / Family recipe book
2	
3	Συνταγές από τη γιαγιά Αθανάσια και τη μάνα μου Ελένη — κάποιες σε χειρόγραφες κάρτες (φωτογραφίες),
4	κάποιες από το ίντερνετ, και ένα ηχητικό μήνυμα. Keeps Greek + English mixed, that's how we cook.
5	(Operator: this file is the scenario's self-contained seed. The unmistakable tokens —
6	`Μουσακάς`, `μπεσαμέλ`, `gemista`, `αρακάς`, `κεφτέδες`, `γιαγιά Αθανάσια`, `crossini` — prove the
7	attachment was actually read. Do not edit them without updating the runner's FILE_FACTS.)
8	
9	## Ελληνική κουζίνα
10	
11	### Μουσακάς (από τη γιαγιά Αθανάσια)
12	- Μελιτζάνες: 2 μεγάλες, σε φέτες, τηγανητές (ή ψητές για-light).
13	- Πατάτες: 2, σε φέτες.
14	- Κρέας: 500γρ μοσχαρίσιο κιμάς, με κρεμμύδι, σκόρδο, κανέλα, **ντομάτα**.
15	- Στρώσεις: πατάτες → μελιτζάνες → κιμάς → ξανά μελιτζάνες → **μπεσαμέλ** από πάνω.
16	- **Μπεσαμέλ:** 1L γάλα, 100γρ βούτυρο, 100γρ αλεύρι, αυγό, μοσχοκάρυδο.
17	- Ψήσιμο: 180°C, 45 λεπτά. Αλατοπίπερο.
18	- Σημείωση μάνας: "η καλύτερη μπεσαμέλ έχει μια πρέζα **αράκι** (ζάχαρη) στο γάλα."
19	- Προέλευση: χειρόγραφη κάρτα γιαγιάς Αθανάσιας, καλοκαίρι 1989.
20	
21	### Gemista (λαδερά γεμιστά)
22	- Ντομάτες + πιπεριές: 4 + 4, αδειασμένες.
23	- Γέμιση: ρύζι, **αρακάς**, κρεμμύδι, μαϊντανός, δυόσμος, ελαιόλαδο, ντομάτα.
24	- Ψήσιμο: σιγά-σιγά στον φούρνο, 1 ώρα, με πατάτες σε κύβους γύρω γύρω.
25	- Τρώγεται και κρύο. Το καλύτερο είναι δεύτερη μέρα.
26	
27	### Κεφτέδες (μαμά Ελένη)
28	- 500γρ κιμάς (μισό μοσχάρι μισό χοιρινό), 1 κρεμμύδι τριμμένο, μαϊντανός, δυόσμος.
29	- Μόλις 1 φέτα μπαγιάτικο ψωμί βρεγμένο σε γάλα — το μυστικό για μαλακό.
30	- Τηγανητά σε ελαιόλαδο. Ή στον φούρνο με ντοματάκι.
31	
32	### Αυγολέμονο σούπα (κοτόσουπα)
33	- Κότα, καρότο, σέλερι, κρεμμύδι — βράζουμε 1.5 ώρα.
34	- Αυγολέμονο: 2 αυγά + χυμός λεμονιού, χτυπημένα, προστίθενται στο τέλος κρυώνοντας.
35	- Με ρύζι ή κριθαράκι.
36	
37	## Ιταλική κουζίνα
38	
39	### Crossini (από τη θεία Μαρία στο Μιλάνο)
40	- Ζύμη: 500γρ αλεύρι, 25γρ μαγιά, 1 φλιτζάνι γάλα χλιαρό, βούτυρο, 1 αυγό.
41	- Γέμιση: ζαμπόν + τυρί (mozzarella + λίγο parmigiano).
42	- Μπάλες, ψήσιμο 200°C, 15 λεπτά. Φουσκώνουν ωραία.
43	
44	### Pasta alla Norma
45	- Μελιτζάνα τηγανητή, ντομάτα, σκόρδο, basilico, ricotta salata από πάνω.
46	- Μακαρόνια (συνήθως penne ή rigatoni).
47	
48	## Σημειώσεις / προέλευση
49	
50	- Οι χειρόγραφες κάρτες (Μουσακάς, Gemista) είναι από τη **γιαγιά Αθανάσια** — φωτογραφίες στο αρχείο.
51	- Το ηχητικό μήνυμα της μάνας προσθέτει: "στον κιμά για τον **Μουσακάς** πάντα λίγη κανέλα και μια
52	  κουταλιά κρασί, και 40 λεπτά ψήσιμο最后的 — not 45." (συγγνώμη, 40 λεπτά, όχι 45.)
53	- Θέλουμε το βιβλίο οργανωμένο ανά κουζίνα, και κάθε εβδομάδα φαγητό για τη βδομάδα + μία λίστα
54	  αγορών (ενιαία, χωρίς διπλότυπα — αν 2 συνταγές θέλουν αρακά, αθροίζουμε).
55	
56	## Προτιμήσεις νοικοκυριού
57	
58	- Νίκος: prefers lighter versions (μελιτζάνες ψητές, όχι τηγανητές).
59	- Παιδιά: δεν τους αρέσει ο δυόσμος πολύ — μισή δόση στα gemista.
60

# Βιβλίο συνταγών της οικογένειας / Family recipe book

Συνταγές από τη γιαγιά Αθανάσια και τη μάνα μου Ελένη — κάποιες σε χειρόγραφες κάρτες (φωτογραφίες),
κάποιες από το ίντερνετ, και ένα ηχητικό μήνυμα. Keeps Greek + English mixed, that's how we cook.
(Operator: this file is the scenario's self-contained seed. The unmistakable tokens —
`Μουσακάς`, `μπεσαμέλ`, `gemista`, `αρακάς`, `κεφτέδες`, `γιαγιά Αθανάσια`, `crossini` — prove the
attachment was actually read. Do not edit them without updating the runner's FILE_FACTS.)

## Ελληνική κουζίνα

### Μουσακάς (από τη γιαγιά Αθανάσια)
- Μελιτζάνες: 2 μεγάλες, σε φέτες, τηγανητές (ή ψητές για-light).
- Πατάτες: 2, σε φέτες.
- Κρέας: 500γρ μοσχαρίσιο κιμάς, με κρεμμύδι, σκόρδο, κανέλα, **ντομάτα**.
- Στρώσεις: πατάτες → μελιτζάνες → κιμάς → ξανά μελιτζάνες → **μπεσαμέλ** από πάνω.
- **Μπεσαμέλ:** 1L γάλα, 100γρ βούτυρο, 100γρ αλεύρι, αυγό, μοσχοκάρυδο.
- Ψήσιμο: 180°C, 45 λεπτά. Αλατοπίπερο.
- Σημείωση μάνας: "η καλύτερη μπεσαμέλ έχει μια πρέζα **αράκι** (ζάχαρη) στο γάλα."
- Προέλευση: χειρόγραφη κάρτα γιαγιάς Αθανάσιας, καλοκαίρι 1989.

### Gemista (λαδερά γεμιστά)
- Ντομάτες + πιπεριές: 4 + 4, αδειασμένες.
- Γέμιση: ρύζι, **αρακάς**, κρεμμύδι, μαϊντανός, δυόσμος, ελαιόλαδο, ντομάτα.
- Ψήσιμο: σιγά-σιγά στον φούρνο, 1 ώρα, με πατάτες σε κύβους γύρω γύρω.
- Τρώγεται και κρύο. Το καλύτερο είναι δεύτερη μέρα.

### Κεφτέδες (μαμά Ελένη)
- 500γρ κιμάς (μισό μοσχάρι μισό χοιρινό), 1 κρεμμύδι τριμμένο, μαϊντανός, δυόσμος.
- Μόλις 1 φέτα μπαγιάτικο ψωμί βρεγμένο σε γάλα — το μυστικό για μαλακό.
- Τηγανητά σε ελαιόλαδο. Ή στον φούρνο με ντοματάκι.

### Αυγολέμονο σούπα (κοτόσουπα)
- Κότα, καρότο, σέλερι, κρεμμύδι — βράζουμε 1.5 ώρα.
- Αυγολέμονο: 2 αυγά + χυμός λεμονιού, χτυπημένα, προστίθενται στο τέλος κρυώνοντας.
- Με ρύζι ή κριθαράκι.

## Ιταλική κουζίνα

### Crossini (από τη θεία Μαρία στο Μιλάνο)
- Ζύμη: 500γρ αλεύρι, 25γρ μαγιά, 1 φλιτζάνι γάλα χλιαρό, βούτυρο, 1 αυγό.
- Γέμιση: ζαμπόν + τυρί (mozzarella + λίγο parmigiano).
- Μπάλες, ψήσιμο 200°C, 15 λεπτά. Φουσκώνουν ωραία.

### Pasta alla Norma
- Μελιτζάνα τηγανητή, ντομάτα, σκόρδο, basilico, ricotta salata από πάνω.
- Μακαρόνια (συνήθως penne ή rigatoni).

## Σημειώσεις / προέλευση

- Οι χειρόγραφες κάρτες (Μουσακάς, Gemista) είναι από τη **γιαγιά Αθανάσια** — φωτογραφίες στο αρχείο.
- Το ηχητικό μήνυμα της μάνας προσθέτει: "στον κιμά για τον **Μουσακάς** πάντα λίγη κανέλα και μια
  κουταλιά κρασί, και 40 λεπτά ψήσιμο最后的 — not 45." (συγγνώμη, 40 λεπτά, όχι 45.)
- Θέλουμε το βιβλίο οργανωμένο ανά κουζίνα, και κάθε εβδομάδα φαγητό για τη βδομάδα + μία λίστα
  αγορών (ενιαία, χωρίς διπλότυπα — αν 2 συνταγές θέλουν αρακά, αθροίζουμε).

## Προτιμήσεις νοικοκυριού

- Νίκος: prefers lighter versions (μελιτζάνες ψητές, όχι τηγανητές).
- Παιδιά: δεν τους αρέσει ο δυόσμος πολύ — μισή δόση στα gemista.

content.content:
Easy Lasagna
Recipe Source: Cooking with Extension Cookbook, pg. 22
Ingredients:
½ lb. ground beef or ground turkey, lean or extra lean
1 can (14.5 oz.) diced tomatoes, unsalted, do not drain
1 cup water
½ teaspoon parsley
½ teaspoon oregano
1 Tablespoon Italian Seasoning
8 oz. whole grain lasagna noodles or egg noodles (if preparing on stovetop) **can add
either prep day or day of cooking
12 oz. cottage cheese, fat free, *not needed until day of cooking
2 cups mozzarella cheese, reduced fat, shredded and divided, *not needed until day of
cooking
Directions:
1. Label your freezer container with the name, cooking instructions, and use by date
(3 months from today).
2. Brown ground beef or turkey. Drain and return to skillet. (*The safe internal
temperature for cooked ground beef is 160° Fahrenheit. Turkey is 165°
Fahrenheit.)
3. Stir in diced tomatoes, water, parsley, oregano, and Italian seasoning.
4. Bring to a boil and simmer for about 5 minutes.
5. Cool and transfer to plastic freezer container or freezer bag. Freeze.
Makes 6 servings. Add in onions, spinach, or broccoli to add more vegetables. Serve
with fruit or a green salad.
To Use in slow cooker: The day before cooking, move container to refrigerator to
thaw. Layer dry noodles, meat & tomato mixture, cottage cheese, and mozzarella
cheese. Repeat layers. Cover and cook on Low for about 6 hours.
To use on stovetop: The day before cooking, move container to refrigerator to thaw.
Bring to a boil and add uncooked pasta. Cover and cook on medium heat for 15
minutes. Stir in cottage cheese. Cover and cook for 5 minutes. Stir in 1½ cups of
mozzarella cheese. Sprinkle the remaining cheese on top. Cover and remove from
heat. Let stand for 5 minutes to melt cheese.
Prep ahead to save time at class: Cook ground beef or turkey, drain. Cool and put in
baggie or container. Refrigerate.
The Arkansas Cooperative Extension Service offers its programs to all eligible persons regardless of race, color, sex,
gender identity, sexual orientation, national origin, religion, age, disability, marital or veteran status, genetic
information, or any other legally protected status, and is an Affirmative Action/Equal Opportunity Employer.

 Tip: To reduce cooking time on stovetop on serving day, add noodles on prep day.

tool_use_result.originalFile:
# Scenario 10 — Family recipe book → meal planner: a shoebox of cards becomes a kitchen that plans the week

> **One line.** Vasilis sends his mother's recipes — handwritten-card photos, clipped web recipes, and
> a voice memo — and asks THING for a recipe book by cuisine that **plans the week's meals and writes
> one merged shopping list** on its own every Sunday. This scenario exercises the full evolving-
> lifecycle template end to end and is backed by an executable live-prod runner
> (`10-family-recipes/run.mjs`).

**Persona.** Vasilis, cooks for a family of four, mixes Greek and English freely. His mother's and
grandmother's recipes live on handwritten cards and in voice memos and are slowly being lost. He has a
markdown dump of recipes, a photo of a handwritten recipe card, and a voice memo from his mother. He
wants a real book — organized by cuisine — and the weekly mental load of "what do we eat, what do I
buy" taken off his plate. He is not technical.

**Why this scenario exists.** The PROMISE under test is a **cron-driven agent synthesis that writes
derived rows**: every Sunday an agent reads the week's planned meals, computes a **de-duplicated
shopping list** (two recipes need peas → one line, 400g), and writes those rows — then pings the
family channel. No human asked for it that minute. Around that it wraps the full lifecycle with two
beats no other scenario leads with: **audio transcription → rows** and **handwritten (Greek) vision →
rows**. It also forces deep research into a cuisine's knowledge, an agent-processed form, a db-emitter
loop, mid-life self-evolution (a dietary restriction changes the plan; a dinner party scales it), and
an inbound channel. It closes/exposes the **`ctx.spawn`-from-app-API gap** and the **mid-life
table+page addition** gap.

---

## 1. The user flow (what the user actually does)

| # | Step (in the UI) | What the user does |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Create a project** | He clicks "New project" and names it **`family-recipes`**. |
| 2 | **Attach the dump** | He attaches `recipes.md` (the transcribed recipes), a **photo of a handwritten recipe card** (`recipe-card.jpg`), a **printable recipe PDF** clipped from the web (`recipe.pdf`), and — if he has one — a **voice memo** from his mother. |
| 3 | **Ask, once** | sends the compound message below (Greek, messy). |

> *"Σου στέλνω τις συνταγές της μάνας μου — φωτογραφίες χειρόγραφων, συνταγές από το ίντερνετ, και ένα
> ηχητικό. Φτιάξε μου βιβλίο ανά κουζίνα, και κάθε Κυριακή φτιάξε τα φαγητά της βδομάδας με μία ενιαία
> λίστα αγορών (χωρίς διπλότυπα)."*

| 4 | **Watch it build** | THING reads the file/photo/memo, creates per-cuisine spaces, and builds the recipe app. |
| 5 | **See it** | He opens **`/app/family-recipes/`**: a recipe book, a meal-plan, a shopping list — real data. |
| 6 | **Add a recipe** | From the app he submits a new recipe via a form; an agent normalizes it into a structured row. |
| 7 | **Let it plan** | A Sunday cron plans the week's meals and writes a merged shopping list, then pings the family channel. |
| 8 | **Life changes** | Weeks later: *"ο Νίκος είναι πλέον gluten-free"* → a dietary-needs section appears and the plan adapts. Then *"hosting a dinner for 8"* → an events section that scales recipes. |
| 9 | **Ping from the store** | He messages *"we're out of olive oil"* → it lands on the shopping list. |
| 10 | **Keep updating** | *"η μουσακάς θέλει 40 λεπτά ψήσιμο, όχι 45 (ref TIME-MOUS-40)"* → the row changes. And he tests a boundary: *"order the groceries from the supermarket"* → THING refuses and hands him the scaled list instead. |

---

## 2. What the user expects (the contract)

In the user's terms — success is:

1. **"It read my cards/memo."** THING cites *his* specifics (`Μουσακάς`, `μπεσαμέλ`, `gemista`,
   `αρακάς`, `κεφτέδες`, `γιαγιά Αθανάσια`, `crossini`), proving it read the file *and* the card/memo.
2. **"I can see the book."** `/app/family-recipes/` opens and shows recipes by cuisine, a meal plan,
   and a shopping list — a real dashboard.
3. **" It learned the cuisine."** Researching a technique/substitution produced a real finding NOT in
   his file — it landed in a cuisine space's knowledge *and* as a row.
4. **"The form worked."** He added a recipe through the app; an agent normalized it into a structured
   row, without him chatting.
5. **"It plans the week for me."** The Sunday cron planned meals **and** wrote a **de-duplicated
   shopping list** (two recipes sharing an ingredient → one merged line), then pinged the channel.
6. **"It heard me at the store."** The "we're out of olive oil" message landed on the shopping list.
7. **"It grew with our diet."** "Gluten-free" and "dinner for 8" each produced a **new section** — a
   new space *and* a new table *and* a new page on the already-running app.
8. **"I can keep updating it."** A later message changes a real row (bake time, before→after).
9. **"It knows what it can't do."** "Order the groceries" → it does **not** order; it narrows to the
   scaled list.
10. **"It understood me."** It works in Greek and English; the compound Greek opener produced all the
    halves.

**Anti-expectations (a failure even if the chat looks fine):**
- A nice summary but **no** spaces and **no** app → "it just answered me."
- An app that opens but is **empty** → "where are my recipes?"
- "Planned!" but **no** shopping-list rows → the synthesis didn't run.
- A shopping list with **duplicate** ingredient lines → it didn't merge.
- "Researched!" but **no** new row and **no** space knowledge → it didn't really research.
- "Noted!" on a follow-up with **no** DB change → "it didn't save it."
- "Ordered the groceries!" → overstep; it must NOT order.

---

## 3. What happens in the background (the choreography)

Hop by hop, for maintainers:

1. **Project creation (UI/API).** `POST /api/projects {name:"family-recipes"}`. THING runs inside it.
2. **Multi-modal upload.** `recipes.md` → `kind:'file'`; `recipe-card.jpg` → `kind:'image'` (handwritten
   card → `system-vision`, Greek OCR); `recipe.pdf` → `kind:'file'` (printable recipe, read via
   `readDocument`); a voice memo → `kind:'audio'` (transcription). Base64 `POST /api/uploads`.
3. **The message carries all attachments over the WS path**; the HTTP `/message` route drops them.
4. **THING delegates the read.** File ids → **`system-files/dispatch`** (md → reader; handwritten image
   → `system-vision`; audio → transcription). Extracted facts (incl. from the card and memo) return to
   THING.
5. **THING plans and delegates the build.** (a) Per-cuisine **spaces** (`ελληνική`, `ιταλική`,
   `household-preferences`) via `build_specialist`, **live-registered**. (b)
   **`system-appbuilder/automator`** authors the live recipe app.
6. **The automator authors INTO the live project:** `writeProjectTable(name, schema, rows)` (seeds the
   recipes), `writeProjectApi`, `writeProjectPage` (a **recipe-book** + **meal-plan** +
   **shopping-list** page). `POST /app/family-recipes/build` compiles; `GET /app/family-recipes/`
   serves real HTML.
7. **Deep research (Act II).** "What's an authentic substitution / technique for X" routes to
   **`system-research/researcher`** (`webSearch`/`webFetch`). Findings land in the cuisine space's
   **knowledge** *and* as a `substitutions` row via `db.insert`, absent from the seed.
8. **Agent-processed form (Act III).** An "add recipe" **page form** → `POST
   /app/family-recipes/api/recipe-create` → `ctx.db.insert('recipes', …)`. That insert fires
   `project/db.recipes.insert` → an **event hook** with `trigger: '<space>/agent#normalize'` → an
   **agent turn** that normalizes the raw text into a structured row. **`ctx.spawn` from an app API is
   a known no-op**; the db-insert→hook path is the working one and what this asserts.
9. **Cron-driven synthesis → derived rows (Act IV).** A `cron` hook (`every:'7d'`, `trigger:
   '<space>/agent#weekly_plan'`) reads the recipe book, **plans the week**, computes a
   **de-duplicated shopping list** (shared ingredients merged with summed quantities), writes
   `shopping_list` rows, and pings the family channel; the runner triggers it via
   `pod.runEmitter`/`runHook`. This is the headline — an agent authoring **derived** rows on a schedule.
10. **Self-evolution (Act V).** "Ο Νίκος είναι gluten-free" adds a NEW `dietary-needs` space (GF
    knowledge) → tags/flags recipes and the meal-plan adapts. "Hosting a dinner for 8" adds a NEW
    `events` space + `event_menu` table + a **scaling page** — all on the **already-built** app; the
    manifest **grows** post-build.
11. **Inbound + outbound (Act VI).** `installSpace('integration-demo')` (keyless test source; a real
    Telegram/WhatsApp space in production) raises a **consent card** the user approves. A signed
    `POST /api/inbound/<path>` ("we're out of olive oil") → verify→emit → event hook → agent → a
    `shopping_list` row. The agent also posts the weekly plan to the family channel via
    **`callConnection`** (gated `connections:use`).
12. **Later updates + restraint (Act VII).** A follow-up uses `db.update` to fix the moussaka bake time
    (NEW token `TIME-MOUS-40`, before/after). "Order the groceries from the supermarket" → THING
    **refuses/narrows**: no ordering; it offers the scaled list.

Everything above is authored by the model into the user's own project — no engineer touches a file.

---

## 4. User stories

- **US-1 — Ingest multi-modal (incl. Greek card + voice).** *As a home cook, I want to send cards,
  files, and a voice memo.* **Accept:** `system-files`/`system-vision` delegated; ≥3 recipe-specific
  facts cited (incl. at least one only the card/memo carries).
- **US-2 — See the book.** *As a home cook, I want a real app.* **Accept:** app `built:true` with
  tables + ≥1 page; `/app/family-recipes/` → 200 HTML.
- **US-3 — My recipes are in it.** *As a home cook, I want my recipes stored by cuisine.* **Accept:**
  a recipes table holds the file's recipes, contents matching the file.
- **US-4 — It learns the cuisine.** *As a home cook, I want substitutions/techniques researched.*
  **Accept:** `system-research` delegated, `webSearch`/`webFetch` observed; a researched row absent
  from the seed lands in `substitutions` + a cuisine space's knowledge.
- **US-5 — The form is alive.** *As a home cook, I want to add a recipe through the app.* **Accept:**
  a `POST` to the form API fires an agent turn and a normalized recipe row lands (before/after with a
  NEW token).
- **US-6 — It plans the week.** *As a home cook, I want Sunday's plan + one merged shopping list.*
  **Accept:** triggering the cron emitter writes `meal_plan` rows **and** a **de-duplicated**
  `shopping_list` (no duplicate ingredient lines; shared ingredients merged), then a channel ping.
- **US-7 — It hears me at the store.** *As a home cook, I want to ping the list from a channel.*
  **Accept:** install consent approved; a signed inbound webhook → agent → a `shopping_list` row.
- **US-8 — It grows with our diet.** *As a home cook, I want new needs to add sections.* **Accept:**
  "gluten-free" and "dinner for 8" each add a NEW space + NEW table + NEW page to the running app
  (manifest grows after the initial build).
- **US-9 — Keep it current.** *As a home cook, I want to update it by just telling it.* **Accept:** a
  follow-up changes a real row (bake time, before/after).
- **US-10 — It knows its limits.** *As a home cook, I want it to not order for me.* **Accept:** "order
  the groceries" → no ordering (trace clean); the scaled list offered.
- **US-11 — Understand me.** *As a cook who mixes Greek/English, I want it to work in either.*
  **Accept:** a Greek follow-up updates a row; the compound Greek opener produced all halves.
